channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF’s AMCA Plan Expands Beyond 120 Jets to a 250-Strong Stealth Fleet
item.description: India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program is increasingly being shaped around a much larger force structure than initially sanctioned. While the current approval framework covers around 120–130 aircraft, equivalent to roughly seven squadrons, long-term planning within the Indian Air Force (IAF) is now oriented toward a 250-aircraft fleet.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Seeks Electromagnetic Launch System for UAVs and Projectiles
item.description: The Indian Navy is pursuing next-generation launch technologies by inviting the development of a compact Electromagnetic Launch System (EMLS) under the DISC 14 innovation challenge. The proposed system is intended to enable the launch of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), loitering munitions, and mission payload projectiles directly from naval platforms without relying on conventional rocket boosters or pneumatic catapult systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From Mission Computers to EW Upgrades: Boeing’s Upgrade Plan Boosts IAF Heavy Airlift Capability
item.description: Boeing has laid out an ambitious long-term roadmap to keep the C-17 Globemaster III operational and relevant until at least 2075, offering a significant capability boost for global operators, including the Indian Air Force (IAF), which fields 11 aircraft and remains the second-largest operator of the platform. The plan effectively extends the lifecycle of the aircraft to over 80 years since its first flight in 1991, underscoring the enduring strategic value of the heavy-lift transport.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Adani Defence showcased updated ULM-ER (ULPGM-V3) with 10 km Stand-Off Precision
item.description: Adani Defence has unveiled the ULM-ER (UAV Launched Missile – Extended Range), the most advanced iteration of its UAV-launched precision-guided munition family, marking a significant evolution in India’s tactical drone strike capabilities. Also designated as ULPGM-V3, the system has been developed in collaboration with DRDO’s Research Centre Imarat (RCI) and is now positioned as a core element in India’s emerging doctrine around armed drones and swarm-enabled warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Astra Mk3 & Virupaksha: How India is Turning the Su-30MKI into a 5th-Gen Stealth Hunter
item.description: The induction of low-observable fighters like the J-35 into the regional battlespace is often framed around the classic “first look, first shot, first kill” paradigm. That logic assumes that stealth platforms will consistently control detection timelines and engagement geometry. The Indian Air Force’s evolving Super Sukhoi construct—pairing a large-aperture AESA radar with a ramjet-powered long-range missile—does not eliminate stealth advantages, but it does compress them to the point where they are no longer decisive on their own. The outcome is a more contested beyond-visual-range (BVR) environment where sensor power, networking, and missile kinematics jointly determine who prevails.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Maraal Aerospace’s ‘Tejasvaan’ Solar UAV Completes Flight Trials, Targets Ultra-Endurance Surveillance for NAMAMI GANGE Mission
item.description: Maraal Aerospace Private Limited has announced the successful flight trials of its solar-powered unmanned aerial vehicle, Tejasvaan, marking a notable step forward in India’s pursuit of sustainable and long-endurance aerial platforms. Designed to operate at the intersection of clean energy aviation and persistent surveillance, Tejasvaan reflects a growing shift toward autonomous systems capable of remaining airborne for extended durations without reliance on conventional fuel-based propulsion.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Operation Sindoor Aftermath: Inside India’s “Iron Dome-Like” Air Defence Shield on the Punjab Border
item.description: Nearly a year after Operation Sindoor, a ground-level assessment along the Punjab border reveals a fundamentally transformed Indian air defence posture—one that is faster, denser, and far more integrated than before. What has emerged is not a single system but a layered, network-centric shield designed to detect, track, assign, and neutralize aerial threats before they penetrate deep into Indian territory. The transformation was forged under pressure, when Pakistan responded to Operation Sindoor with a coordinated wave of drones and missiles, including threats aimed beyond military targets and into civilian areas.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO unveils Advanced Armoured Platforms (Tracked & Wheeled)
item.description: The Advanced Armoured Platforms (Tracked and Wheeled), designed and developed by Vehicles Research & Development Establishment, were unveiled by Secretary Department of Defence (R&D) & Chairman DRDO Dr Samir V Kamat at the premises of the DRDO’s laboratory in Ahilyanagar, Maharashtra on April 25, 2026. The platforms have been developed to meet the emerging operational requirements of the Defence Forces.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NSTL and Indian Complete Hydrodynamic Performance Assessment for Frontline Indian Naval Ships
item.description: Naval Science & Technological Laboratory(NSTL), DRDO in collaboration with Warship Design Bureau of Indian Navy has successfully completed Hydrodynamic Performance Assessment and Model Testing of Frontline Indian Naval Ship Project. Comprehensive capabilities were demonstrated in Hull hydrodynamic, CFD based simulation and experimental model testing, covering critical parameters such as resistance, propulsion, sea keeping and manoeuvrability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India rejects Bangladesh media reports alleging the presence of Indian-made ammunition in the Chittagong Hill Tracts
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has dismissed reports in Bangladeshi media alleging that Indian-made ammunition was found or used in the Chittagong Hill Tracts (CHT). At a weekly briefing on 23 April 2026, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal described these claims as “unverified” and declined to comment further on them.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Spillage to open reactor’ phase kicks off at Kudankulam nuclear plant’s unit 3
item.description: The Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project’s unit three witnessed the commencement of the “spillage to open reactor” phase on Saturday, marking a significant development in the ongoing construction of the project, a statement said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Telangana CM flags off Skyroot’s Vikram-1, India’s first private rocket
item.description: Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy on Saturday flagged off the flight hardware of Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 — India’s first privately developed orbital rocket here — marking the rocket’s formal departure to the launch site at Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: China hints fourth aircraft carrier may be nuclear-powered in new video
item.description: A latest video released by the Chinese navy highlighting China’s blue-water naval ambitions has sparked speculation that its fourth aircraft carrier currently being built will be a nuclear-powered one. The video film ‘Into The Deep’ was released on Wednesday to mark the 77th anniversary of the founding of the People’s Liberation Army Navy showcased Chinese navy’s transition from maritime ambitions and coastal defences.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Inauguration of OTM Complex at Puducherry
item.description: Director General Indian Coast Guard, DG Paramesh Sivamani inaugurated the state-of-the-art OTM Administrative Complex of Coast Guard District Headquarters No. 13 at Puducherry on 25 April 2026. The inauguration marks a significant milestone in strengthening India’s maritime security architecture along the eastern seaboard. The newly constructed complex reflects the vision of a “Modern Force, Modern Infrastructure” and is designed to enhance operational efficiency and administrative capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India advises nationals in Mali to stay indoors, exercise caution due to security developments
item.description: India on Saturday advised its nationals in Mali to remain highly vigilant, exercise utmost caution and stay indoors due to recent security developments in the West African nation.
item.dc:creator: admin
